title: Letter Combinations of a Phone Number
prompt:
Given a string containing digits from 2-9 inclusive, return all possible letter combinations that the number could represent on a phone keypad. Return the answer in any order, but sorted lexicographically.

The mapping of digit to letters (same as on telephone buttons) is:
2 → abc, 3 → def, 4 → ghi, 5 → jkl, 6 → mno, 7 → pqrs, 8 → tuv, 9 → wxyz

Note that 1 does not map to any letters. If the input is an empty string, return an empty list.

Example: digits = "23" → ["ad","ae","af","bd","be","bf","cd","ce","cf"].
constraints: digits[i] is a digit in the range ['2', '9']

notesForAI.coreIdea: Use a mapping from digit to letters. Backtrack: for each digit, try each mapped letter, append to current combination, recurse on remaining digits. Base case: when all digits are processed, add combination to results.
commonBugs: Returning [''] instead of [] for empty input
Incorrect digit-to-letter mapping (7 has pqrs, 9 has wxyz — 4 letters each)
Not handling the empty string edge case
notesForAI.expectedComplexity: O(4^n * n) where n is the number of digits
